steps.title: Map the text's three-part structure
steps.content: The passage moves through three beats: (1) an obstacle — the NCP is discernible only at night; (2) an inspiration — researchers were inspired by the navigational strategies of insects and birds that use skylight polarization; (3) a solution — researchers mimicked that ability with a polarimetric camera that captures polarization patterns to locate the NCP in daytime. Every correct description must capture all three beats in order.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice against the text
steps.content:
Check which description accurately matches the obstacle $\Rightarrow$ inspiration $\Rightarrow$ solution structure:
  • Choice A — It illustrates how most navigational tools utilize the NCP, recounts how researchers discovered that certain animals are able to navigate without using the NCP, and then proposes that this discovery could be used to avoid problems in navigation associated with reliance on the NCP.: the text never says most navigational tools use the NCP, and it never says researchers discovered animals navigate without it — animals use polarization to find the NCP. ✗
  • Choice B — It presents a celestial-based method of navigation, enumerates the comparative benefits of an alternative method used by certain animals that is based on an unrelated natural occurrence, and then indicates how researchers assessed the relative accuracy of the two methods.: the text does not compare the accuracy of animal vs. human methods, and the animal strategy is not "unrelated" to the NCP. ✗
  • Choice C — It explains how the NCP is typically located, emphasizes a key difference between how humans and certain animals use the NCP for navigation, and then suggests an alternative way of using the NCP to improve existing navigational instruments.: the text says nothing about how humans typically locate the NCP, and it does not propose augmenting existing navigational instruments. ✗
  • Choice D — It notes an obstacle to observing an astronomical phenomenon, mentions a navigational ability of certain animals that inspired a solution to that obstacle, and then explains how researchers used an optical device to mimic that ability.: matches all three beats exactly — obstacle (NCP only visible at night), inspiration (animal polarization navigation), and solution (polarimetric camera mimics that ability). ✓
D is correct.
